svg.aria-label: The same question answered without and with the Cognitive Lenses engine
svg.text: The same question, with and without the engine
“Our product API is getting slow (p95 ~900 ms). Should we add a Redis cache in front of Postgres?
Team of three, paid tier launches next month.”
WITHOUT · DIRECT ANSWER
“Yes — add managed Redis, cache-aside,
TTL 60–300 s, invalidate on writes.”
WHAT IT NEVER ASKED
Is the database actually the bottleneck?
Is launch month the time for new infrastructure?
What do stale reads cost once customers pay?
Who sees whose data if cache keys ignore tenants?
Competent, actionable — and it accepted the premise.
WITH · COGNITIVE LENSES (5 LENSES → CONSENSUS)
“Not yet — locate the bottleneck first, and don’t ship
a new stateful dependency in your launch month.”
WHAT THE LENSES CAUGHT
3 lenses converge: the bottleneck was never measured
Risk: two launches sharing one blast radius
Systems: stale entitlements become billing disputes
Security: tenant-scoped keys, or customers see each other
Same caching plan if evidence demands it — sequenced after proof.
Full worked run: examples/with-vs-without.md · explicit engine costs ≈ 10–15× the tokens of the direct answer — see the benchmark for when that trade pays.
